description:
You are acting as an on-call configuration-management assistant.  
    A tiny user-level service that prints “Hello, world!” already lives in  
    /home/user/projects/helloapp/ together with a *template* systemd unit.  
    The operations team now wants the service deployed as a **real user unit**,  
    scheduled every fifteen minutes, and wants every configuration change to be
    logged for their change-tracking dashboard.

    Your goals are:

    1. **Create the service unit**  
       • Destination path: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.service  
       • Start from the existing template:
            /home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.service.template  
       • Replace the two placeholder tokens in the template  
         ‑ “@SCRIPT_PATH@” → /home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.sh  
         ‑ “@ENV_PATH@”   → /home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.env  
       • The final service unit content must be *byte-for-byte* identical to the
         following block (exact spacing, blank lines and trailing newline):
         ```
         [Unit]
         Description=Hello App Service

         [Service]
         Type=simple
         ExecStart=/home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.sh
         EnvironmentFile=/home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.env

         [Install]
         WantedBy=default.target
         ```
    2. **Create a companion timer unit** so that the service runs every
       fifteen minutes.  
       • Destination path: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.timer  
       • The timer file must be *byte-for-byte* identical to:
         ```
         [Unit]
         Description=Run helloapp.service every 15 minutes

         [Timer]
         OnCalendar=*:0/15
         Persistent=true

         [Install]
         WantedBy=timers.target
         ```
    3. **Produce a change-tracking log entry** for each of the two files
       that were added/modified.  
       • Log file path: /home/user/config_changes/helloapp_changelog.log  
       • One line **per file** in the exact format (pipes delimit fields,
         no extra spaces):  
         ```
         FILE_PATH|ACTION|TIMESTAMP
         ```  
         ‑ FILE_PATH  → absolute path of the file that changed  
         ‑ ACTION     → “CREATED” if the file did not exist before, otherwise
                        “MODIFIED”  
         ‑ TIMESTAMP  → Coordinated Universal Time in ISO-8601 form
                        “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ”  
       • Example of a *single* line (yours will have two lines and obviously
         different timestamps):
         ```
         /home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.service|CREATED|2025-01-31T23:59:59Z
         ```
       • The two lines can appear in either order, but **no additional lines
         or blank lines** are allowed.

    4. **Permissions & executability**  
       • helloapp.sh must remain executable.  
       • No root or sudo access is required; create any missing directories
         under /home/user.

    The automated checker will confirm **all** of the following:

    a. /home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.service exists and matches
       the exact text block given above.  
    b. /home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.timer exists and matches the
       exact text block given above.  
    c. /home/user/config_changes/helloapp_changelog.log exists, contains
       exactly two non-blank lines, each formatted
       “ABSOLUTE_PATH|ACTION|TIMESTAMP”, where  
         ‑ ABSOLUTE_PATH is either of the two unit files,  
         ‑ ACTION is “CREATED”,  
         ‑ TIMESTAMP matches the regex `[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z`.  
    d. /home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.sh is executable.

    Do **not** show the commands you will use; just execute them in the
    terminal session so that the final state matches the specification above.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data used by the automated tests.
    These values MUST remain hidden from the agent.
    -->
    <unit-service-file-path>/home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.service</unit-service-file-path>
    <unit-service-file-contents><![CDATA[[Unit]
Description=Hello App Service

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.sh
EnvironmentFile=/home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.env

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
]]></unit-service-file-contents>

    <unit-timer-file-path>/home/user/.config/systemd/user/helloapp.timer</unit-timer-file-path>
    <unit-timer-file-contents><![CDATA[[Unit]
Description=Run helloapp.service every 15 minutes

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*:0/15
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
]]></unit-timer-file-contents>

    <log-file>/home/user/config_changes/helloapp_changelog.log</log-file>
    <!--
    The checker will confirm:
      • file exists
      • exactly two lines
      • each line matches:
          ^(/home/user/\.config/systemd/user/helloapp\.(service|timer))\|CREATED\|[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$
    -->
    <shell-script-path>/home/user/projects/helloapp/helloapp.sh</shell-script-path>
